children.heading: .—Eligibility of Members
children.label: a .—Eligibility of Members
children.text: A member of the National Guard who, although not in Federal service, is called or ordered to duty in response to a federally declared disaster or national emergency shall be permitted to use commissary stores and MWR retail facilities during the period of such duty on the same basis as members of the armed forces on active duty.

children.heading: .—Eligibility of Dependents
children.label: b .—Eligibility of Dependents
children.text: A dependent of a member of the National Guard who is permitted under subsection (a) to use commissary stores and MWR retail facilities shall be permitted to use such stores and facilities, during the same period as the member, on the same basis as dependents of members of the armed forces on active duty.

children.heading: .—Federally declared disaster
children.label: 1 .—Federally declared disaster
children.text: The term “federally declared disaster” means a disaster or other situation for which a Presidential declaration of major disaster is issued under section 401 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ().42 U.S.C. 5170

children.heading: .—MWR retail facilities
children.label: 2 .—MWR retail facilities
children.text: The term “MWR retail facilities” has the meaning given that term in section 1063(e) of this title.1See References in text note below.1

children.heading: .—National emergency
children.label: 3 .—National emergency
children.text: The term “national emergency” means a national emergency declared by the President or Congress.
